question_text: A car is travelling along a horizontal road and experiences four forces: weight (5000 N downward), normal reaction force (5000 N upward), an engine thrust of 1500 N forward, and friction of 1200 N backward. Calculate the net force acting on the car and state which forces are contact forces.
correct_answer: Net force = 300 N forward. Contact forces: normal reaction force (5000 N) and friction (1200 N). Non-contact force: weight (5000 N). The engine thrust (1500 N) is also a contact force.
explanation: To find the net force, you only consider forces in the same direction: 1500 N forward – 1200 N backward = 300 N forward. The vertical forces (weight and normal reaction) are balanced so they cancel out. Contact forces require the car/road/engine to be touching (normal reaction, friction, thrust), while weight is a non-contact gravitational force.
